acceptance_targets: [CH17_AWARD_OVERCERTIFIED_ENDING] Pass if, after the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:483 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:499, the chapter reaches the six-o'clock choice from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:591 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:609 without any explicit summary line serving the same function as snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:543, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:553, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:693, or snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:713. Fail if the revised ending still explains in abstract terms what the bequest, the objects, and Tommy's reaction already make clear.

[P-01_chapter_17] Paul's speeches at 6575 and 6627 use contracted forms. No antithetical balance between sentences (the giving/that-is-all/giving mirror structure is dissolved). The speeches read as a man making a decision under pressure, not as prepared rhetoric.
[RED-26] Ch 17's description of Tommy in the chair no longer shares 10+ consecutive content words with Ch 16 line 5976.
[RED-27] Ch 17 contains fewer than 46 sentences beginning with 'He' (under 7% of chapter sentences).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:483 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:499, the chapter reaches the six-o'clock choice from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:591 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:609 without any explicit summary line serving the same function as snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:543, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:553, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:693, or snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:713. Fail if the revised ending still explains in abstract terms what the bequest, the objects, and Tommy's reaction already make clear.
findings.problem: The chapter's best material is concrete: Tommy physically buckles when he learns Martin left him the house, then inventories the green-room maintenance as the scale of Martin's twenty-two-year arrangement hits him. After that point, the chapter keeps translating the same insight into thesis language and certification ('It was love too,' 'kind and mean at the same time,' 'running a system,' 'loved each other in different wrong ways'). The meaning is already legible on the page; restating it softens the ending's compression and keeps the chapter below award-pass force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the post-bequest discussion at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:543 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:553 so Tommy's realization stays in behavior, silence, or object-specific language rather than abstract diagnosis. Then compress or quiet the verification coda at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:679, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:693, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:713. Preserve the state-changing ultimatum at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:591 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:609, but remove lines whose only job is to paraphrase the meaning already carried by the bequest break and the green-room objects.

findings.acceptance_test: Paul's speeches at 6575 and 6627 use contracted forms. No antithetical balance between sentences (the giving/that-is-all/giving mirror structure is dissolved). The speeches read as a man making a decision under pressure, not as prepared rhetoric.
findings.evidence: Line 6575: 'Meaning I am not spending the next twenty years adjusting around what you did. I am not taking your keys and making calls and helping you keep a lid on it and pretending that's friendship. Martin made his choice. I'm making mine.' Line 6627: 'I am giving you the night. That is all I am giving you.'
findings.problem: Same pattern as Chapter 16: anaphoric uncontracted negation ('I am not... I am not...') followed by a contracted pivot ('I'm making mine'). At 6627, the antithetical structure ('I am giving you X / That is all I am giving you') is balanced to the syllable — this is compositional craft, not speech. Paul is giving Tommy the most consequential ultimatum of the novel, and the diction places the reader in a courtroom rather than on a deck between old friends. The contracted 'I'm' buried between the uncontracted 'I am' clauses at 6575 makes the formality choice inconsistent rather than emphatic.
findings.rewrite_direction: At 6575: 'I'm not spending the next twenty years adjusting around what you did. I'm not taking your keys and making calls and keeping a lid on it and calling that friendship.' — contracts throughout, replaces 'pretending that's' with 'calling that' for harder landing. At 6627: 'I'm giving you tonight. That's all I've got.' — shorter, harder, more consistent with Paul's tightened-under-stress profile. The ultimatum's force should come from brevity and finality, not from formal diction.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 17's description of Tommy in the chair no longer shares 10+ consecutive content words with Ch 16 line 5976.
findings.evidence: Ch 16 line 5976: 'Tommy sat outside in Martin's chair, broad shoulders rounded inward, a beer warming on the armrest he wasn't touching.' Ch 17 line 5999: 'Tommy was in the Adirondack chair closest to the railing, Martin's chair whether anybody called it that or not, with a beer balanced on the armrest he was not touching.'
findings.problem: An 11-content-word near-verbatim passage spans consecutive chapters: 'Martin's chair... a beer [warming/balanced] on the armrest he was[n't] [not] touching.' The image is essentially identical — Tommy sitting in Martin's chair with an untouched beer on the armrest.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch 17, rephrase Tommy's position to avoid echoing Ch 16's specific construction. The beer and the chair can appear without the identical 'on the armrest he was not touching' formula. Alternatively, cut the untouched-beer-on-armrest detail from one chapter.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 17 contains fewer than 46 sentences beginning with 'He' (under 7% of chapter sentences).
findings.evidence: Ch 17: 78 sentences beginning with 'He' out of 652 total sentences = 12.0%. Manuscript-wide 'He' rate: 476/7395 = 6.44%. Ch 17 is the worst offender, nearly double the manuscript average.
findings.problem: Ch 17 (Paul POV) opens 12.0% of its sentences with 'He' — 78 of 652 sentences. This is the highest rate of any chapter and nearly double the manuscript-wide average. The pattern creates rhythmic monotony in Paul's extended retrospective chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 30 of the 78 'He' openers in Ch 17 by using clause inversions, participial phrases, or object-first constructions. Target bringing the chapter below 7% (max 45 'He' openers).

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
